name: Tamarind Pulp Infusion
description: A magnificent indigenous dryland tree. The acidic brown fruit pulp is rich in tartaric acid, citric acid, and essential potassium minerals, acting as a highly effective cooling febrifuge for heat exhaustion and a natural oral rehydration electrolyte enhancer.
preparation: Rehydration infusion: Steep 10g of clean fruit pulp in 500ml of warm water for 15 minutes. Mash, strain, and administer warm concurrently with standard ORS solutions during diarrheal volume depletion.
safetyAlert: Exceptionally safe. Widely consumed as a nutritious fruit beverage across East Africa.
interactions: None known. Highly beneficial when combined with standard hydration protocols.
